originalTitle: Kenya to charge students with murder over deadly school fire
title: A Sad Story and How We Can All Be Safe at School!
content:
Hello, My Wonderful Friends!

Hello, my wonderful friends! Your kid-friendly reporter is here. Sometimes, we hear news that makes our hearts feel a little heavy. Today, I want to talk about something sad that happened in Kenya, but also about how we can all learn to keep safe. It's about a school, just like yours, called Utumishi Girls' School.

What Happened at School?

Last month, at this school, there was a big fire. The fire happened in a place called a 'dormitory.' Do you know what a dormitory is? It's like a really big bedroom where many students sleep together when they live at school. Imagine you and all your friends sleeping in one big room – that's a dormitory! This fire was very, very sad. Sixteen girls, who were like big sisters, aged between 15 and 18 years old, were sleeping in the dormitory. The fire made them very, very sick, and sadly, they could not wake up. This is a very sad 'tragedy' – that means a terrible event where people get hurt or die. Everyone in Kenya, and even all over Africa, felt very sad for these girls and their families.

Looking for Answers (Investigation)

When something very sad and puzzling like this happens, grown-ups try very hard to find out why. This is called an 'investigation.' It's like being a detective! They look for clues, they talk to people, and they try to understand exactly what caused the fire. They want to know so that it doesn't happen again. It's very important to find answers. The grown-ups, like the police and other leaders, are still working hard on this investigation. They want to make sure they know the truth. They are asking many questions to put all the pieces of the puzzle together.

A Serious Talk (The Court and Charges)

Now, this is a tricky part to explain, but I will try my best for you. During the investigation, the grown-ups found some things that made them think maybe, just maybe, some other students helped start the fire on purpose. This is a very serious idea. When grown-ups think someone did something very wrong on purpose, they might 'charge' them. This means they say, 'We think you did this bad thing, and we need to talk about it in a special place.' This special place is called a 'court.' A court is where a judge and other smart people listen to all the clues and try to figure out what really happened and who is responsible. The very serious word you might hear is 'murder.' Murder means making someone die on purpose. Right now, some students from the school are going to go to this court. But remember, this does NOT mean they *did* it. It means the grown-ups are going to look very carefully at all the clues to find out if they did. It's like when you try to figure out who ate the last cookie – you need to look at all the crumbs and listen to everyone before you know for sure! It's very important to find out the truth fairly.

Keeping Everyone Safe (Fire Safety)

This sad story reminds us all how important it is to be safe, especially from fire. Fire can be very useful for cooking and keeping warm, but it can also be very dangerous if we are not careful. Our schools, whether you live there or just go for the day, have 'safety rules.' These rules are like special instructions to keep everyone safe. One very important rule is about fire safety. We must know what to do if there is a fire. Do you know what to do? One thing schools do is practice 'fire drills.' A fire drill is when everyone pretends there is a fire, and they practice walking calmly out of the building to a safe spot. It's not a real fire, but it helps everyone know what to do if a real fire happens. It's like practicing a dance so you know all the steps! Did you know? Many schools have special red bottles on the walls called 'fire extinguishers.' These bottles have special stuff inside that can put out small fires quickly. But only grown-ups should use them! It's important to listen to your teachers and grown-ups when they talk about fire safety. They will teach you: * How to get out of a building safely. * To never play with matches or lighters. * To tell a grown-up right away if you see fire or smell smoke. Knowing these things can help save lives, just like being a superhero!

Helping Each Other

When sad things happen, it's also a time for everyone to come together and help. The families of the girls who passed away need lots of love and support. Friends, neighbors, and people all over Kenya are sending messages of comfort and help. It's important to be kind and caring to people who are sad. We can show our care by listening, giving hugs, and doing small acts of kindness. This is how we build a strong community – by helping each other when times are tough. Did you know? In many African cultures, 'Ubuntu' is a special word that means 'I am because we are.' It reminds us that we are all connected and should always help each other.

Learning and Growing Stronger

From every sad event, we can learn something important. This fire teaches us that we must always be careful and make sure our schools are the safest places they can be. Grown-ups will now look even more closely at all schools to check their fire safety plans. They will make sure all dormitories are super safe. It's a big job, but everyone wants to make sure that children in Kenya, and all over Africa, can go to school and learn and play without worrying. Learning is so important for your future, and schools should always be safe places to grow. Did you know? Kenya is a beautiful country in East Africa, famous for its amazing wildlife and kind people! Schools there are very important for helping children grow up to be amazing adults.

A Message of Hope

Even though this story is sad, we can always find hope. Hope means believing that good things will happen and that we can make things better. We hope that the truth will be found about the fire at Utumishi Girls' School. And we hope that all schools will become even safer. Remember to always listen to your teachers about safety rules. You are all very special, and your safety is the most important thing. Let's all work together to make our schools and homes safe and happy places for everyone!
summary: A very sad fire happened at Utumishi Girls' School in Kenya, and now grown-ups are looking for answers and teaching everyone how to stay safe from fire.
whyMatters: This story matters because it helps us remember to always be careful, listen to safety rules, and help each other so that all schools can be safe and happy places for children to learn and grow.

originalTitle: Sexual violence increasingly used as 'weapon of war' in Sudan, UN says
title: Sad News from Sudan: Being Unfair is Making People Scared
content:
Hello, Friends from Africa!

Hello, amazing young friends! Your friend, the kid-friendly news reporter, is here. I want to talk about something important happening in a beautiful country in Africa called Sudan. Sometimes, grown-ups have big problems, and it’s my job to help you understand them simply. Today, we're talking about something very sad and unfair, but it’s important to know about it, and know that people are trying to help.

What is Happening in Sudan?

Sudan is a big country in Africa, just like many of our homes! It has lovely people, amazing animals, and big, wide lands. But right now, some grown-ups in Sudan are having a very big fight. When grown-ups fight, it's called a 'conflict' or sometimes a 'war'. This fight has been going on for a little while, and it makes many people very, very sad and scared. Homes get broken, schools sometimes close, and it’s hard for people to get food and water. Did you know? Sudan is the third-largest country in Africa by land area! It has the famous River Nile flowing through it.

What Does 'Being Unfair' Mean Here?

Now, here’s the really sad part. In this fight, some grown-ups are doing things that are incredibly unfair and hurtful to other grown-ups. The United Nations, which is like a big club of countries trying to help the world, says that these unfair things are happening a lot more now. They are calling it 'sexual violence'. What does that mean for you to understand? It means that people are being hurt in a very special, very mean way. Their bodies are not being respected. They are made to feel unsafe, scared, and very sad. It’s like someone taking your favourite toy and breaking it on purpose, but much, much worse, because it happens to a person’s body and heart. These actions are never, ever okay, and they are never the fault of the person who gets hurt.

Why Do Grown-Ups Do Such Mean Things?

This is a hard question, but the big club of countries (the UN) says that these very unfair and hurtful actions are being used as a 'weapon of war'. Imagine a 'weapon' usually means a gun or a sword, right? But sometimes, grown-ups use very bad actions as a 'weapon' too. They do these mean things to make people so scared and hurt that they give up, or leave their homes, or stop trying to live their normal lives. It’s a way to make a whole community feel terrified and powerless. Did you know? The word 'community' means a group of people living in the same place or having a particular characteristic in common. Your school is a community!

Who is the UN?

I mentioned the United Nations, or the UN. So, who are they? Imagine if all the countries in the world had a giant meeting club. That’s kind of what the UN is! It’s an organization where almost all countries come together to talk about big problems and try to find peaceful solutions. They want to make the world a safer, fairer place for everyone, especially for children. They have people who work very hard to help countries that are having trouble, like Sudan. They send aid, they talk to leaders, and they try to protect people. Did you know? The UN was created after a big war many years ago, so that countries could talk instead of fight.

What is the UN Saying?

The UN has a special group that looks after children and also a group that looks after everyone’s safety. These groups are very worried. They are saying that the amount of unfair and hurtful actions (sexual violence) in Sudan is getting much, much bigger. They used words like 'unprecedented', which means it’s never been this bad before. And they said these bad actions are done to make whole towns and villages feel so scared they can’t live normally anymore. This makes the UN and many good people around the world very sad and determined to help.

Why is This So Important?

When people are hurt in such an unfair way, it breaks their hearts, it makes them feel unsafe in their own bodies, and it makes them fear for their lives and the lives of their families. It makes it hard for them to go to school, or work, or even just laugh and play. It breaks families apart, and it stops communities from growing strong. Everyone, no matter where they live or how old they are, deserves to feel safe, respected, and happy. When these bad things happen, it takes away those very important rights. We call these 'human rights' – the rights every person has just for being a human. Did you know? There are over 7 billion people in the world, and every single one of them deserves to be treated with kindness and respect!

Who Gets Hurt the Most?

Sadly, in these kinds of sad situations, women and girls are often hurt the most by these very unfair and mean actions. This is extra sad because women and girls are strong, smart, and important parts of every family and community. They deserve to be protected and to grow up feeling safe and powerful, just like boys and men. When they are hurt, it hurts everyone.

What Can We Do?

Even though we are far away, and you are still young, there are things we can do. First, we can learn. Knowing what’s happening helps us to understand the world. Second, we can wish for peace. Wishing for peace means hoping very, very strongly that the fighting stops and people can live safely again. Third, we can be kind to everyone around us. When we show kindness, we help make our own communities stronger and safer. We can also tell our grown-ups that this is happening and ask them what they are doing to help. Many grown-ups are working very hard to stop this and help the people in Sudan. Did you know? Even a small act of kindness, like sharing a toy or saying a nice word, can make a big difference in someone's day!

Wishing for Peace

So, while it's a very sad thing happening in Sudan, remember that many good people, like the UN and helpers from all over the world, are trying their very best to stop these unfair actions and bring peace to the people there. Let’s all send our hopes and wishes for peace and safety to the children and grown-ups in Sudan. Every person deserves to live without fear and to be treated with love and respect. Let’s hope for a brighter, safer tomorrow for everyone in Sudan. Stay kind, stay curious, and always wish for peace! Your Kid-Friendly News Reporter.

originalTitle: 'VAR went for a coffee' - should Ghana have had penalty against England?
title: A Big Football Match for Ghana! Did VAR Help?
content:
Hello, hello, my super friends! It's your favorite reporter from Africa, ready with some super exciting news for you! Get ready to clap your hands and cheer! Guess what? There was a really big football match! Our friends from Ghana, a wonderful country in Africa, played against a strong team from England! It was super, super exciting!

What is Football?

Do you know what football is? It’s a game where two teams run around on a big green field. They try to kick a round ball into the other team’s net! It's like a big dance with your feet, but with a ball! Everyone runs, kicks, and tries their very best to score a goal. Scoring a goal is like getting a point! The team with the most goals wins the game. Millions and millions of people all over the world love to play and watch football. Maybe you play football too with your friends? It makes you strong and happy! Did you know? Football is the most popular sport in the whole wide world! More people watch football than any other game!

Ghana Plays England!

So, in this special game, our amazing team from Ghana played against England. Ghana is a beautiful country right here in Africa! And England is a country far, far away across the sea. Both teams have super talented players! They are very good at kicking the ball and running super fast. Everyone watching was cheering very loudly for their favorite team! The players from Ghana wore their special team shirts. They ran up and down the pitch, which is what we call the football field. They wanted to win very much!

A Game with No Goals!

Guess what happened at the end of the game? The game finished with no goals! It was 0 goals for Ghana, and 0 goals for England. Wow, that’s quite rare! We call this a 'goalless draw'. It means that neither team scored any goals. So, the game ended with both teams getting a point. It’s a bit like sharing your sweets with a friend! Sometimes, games end like this. It can make us a little bit sad because we love to see goals! But it also means that both teams played very well to stop the other team from scoring. They were great at defending! Did you know? A football field is called a 'pitch'! And the special shoes players wear are called 'boots'!

What is a Penalty?

Now, let's talk about something very important in football called a 'penalty'. Can you say 'penalty'? It’s a very special kick in football. If a player from one team does something wrong inside a special box near their own goal, the other team gets a penalty kick! It’s like a super free shot at the goal! The player takes the ball to a special spot, far from the goal. They kick the ball, and only one person from the other team – the goalkeeper – can try to stop it. It’s a big, big chance to score a goal!

Did Ghana Get a Penalty?

During the game between Ghana and England, something happened! Some people thought that a player from England made a mistake inside their penalty box. And because of that, many thought Ghana should have gotten a penalty kick! Oh my! A penalty could have helped Ghana score a goal. A goal from a penalty could have even helped Ghana win the whole game! Everyone was watching very closely to see what would happen.

What is VAR? Meet the Super Helper!

This is where a super helper comes in! It’s called VAR. Can you say VAR? It stands for Video Assistant Referee. Wow, that's a big name! But it's really simple! Imagine a referee, who is the person in charge of the game. They run around the field and make all the decisions. But sometimes, it's hard to see everything perfectly, right? Things happen very fast! So, VAR is like having another referee watching the game on many TV screens! They have many cameras showing every single part of the game from all different directions. It's like having super eyes! If something big happens, like a possible penalty, the VAR helper can tell the referee to come and look at the TV screen! The referee can then watch the replay again and again. It's like getting a second opinion! The referee watches the replay and then makes the very best decision. This helps to make sure the game is fair for everyone. It’s like a superpower for the referee! Did you know? The first VAR system was used in a major football match in 2016! It's still quite new and helps make games fairer!

The Big Decision!

In our Ghana versus England game, the VAR helper looked very, very closely at the possible penalty incident. Everyone was waiting! Would Ghana get a penalty? Or would the referee say no? After looking at the TV screen carefully, the referee made a decision. The referee decided that it was not a penalty for Ghana. Oh! That made some Ghana fans a little bit sad. When we are sad because something didn't go the way we wanted, we call it being 'frustrated'. It's okay to feel that way sometimes. But it’s important to always be fair and respect the decisions of the referee and VAR, even if they are tough.

Playing Fair is Important!

Even if the decisions are hard, playing fair is the most important thing in football. Everyone tries their best, and sometimes things don't go your way. But we learn to keep trying and keep smiling! Our team from Ghana played with big, brave hearts! They ran, they kicked, they showed so much courage and skill. We are so, so proud of them for trying their best! Did you know? Ghana is famous for its delicious cocoa, which is used to make yummy chocolate! Maybe the players get a chocolate treat after a tough game!

Let's Keep Cheering for Africa!

This game showed everyone that teams from Africa are strong and can play super well against anyone in the world! We must always cheer loudly for our teams! Football is all about fun, making friends, and trying your absolute hardest. No matter what the score, we celebrate the effort and the joy of playing the game together! So, let’s keep playing, keep cheering, and keep believing in our amazing teams from Africa! They make us all so very proud! Go Africa, go Ghana!
summary: Ghana played a football match against England that ended in a 0-0 draw, and there was a big talk about whether Ghana should have gotten a penalty kick, which the special video helper (VAR) decided against.
whyMatters: This matters because it shows how important fairness is in football games, even when decisions are tricky, and it reminds us to cheer for our amazing African teams no matter what!

title: Bosnia win 3-2, knock out Qatar to keep alive hopes of World Cup round of 32

content: Madibo's tackle on Kone in the team's Group B match left the Canadian player with a broken leg.

originalTitle: Katz: Israel will not withdraw from Lebanon even if US demands it
title: Friends and Neighbors: Talking About Staying Safe
content:
Hello, Wonderful Friends!

Jambo, amazing kids from all over Africa and the world! It's your friendly reporter here, bringing you news that's easy to understand. We talk about big things happening in the world, but we break them down into little pieces, just for you. Today, we're going to talk about something happening between two countries far away. It's a story about neighbors and keeping safe. Sometimes, countries need to talk about how to keep everyone safe and happy. This story is about two countries called Israel and Lebanon. They are neighbors, just like you might have neighbors next door!

Meet Our Neighbors: Israel and Lebanon

Imagine a beautiful part of the world called the Middle East. That's where Israel and Lebanon live side-by-side. They share a border, which is like a line on the ground that shows where one country stops and another begins. Lebanon is a country with lovely mountains and a blue sea. Israel is also a country with a long history and many special places. Both countries have many people who live there, go to school, play, and work, just like us! Did you know? Countries have borders to show where their land is, just like how your house has a fence or a wall to show your garden! It helps everyone know where their space is.

What Does "Defence Minister" Mean?

In every country, there are people who help make important decisions. One very important job is called "Defence Minister." This person is like the main helper for the country's army and police. Their biggest job is to make sure all the people in their country are safe. They think about how to protect homes, schools, and families. So, when we hear about "Defence Minister Israel Katz," we know he is a very important person whose job is to keep Israel safe. Did you know? In many African countries, we also have brave Defence Ministers who work hard to protect everyone in our homes and villages. They help make sure we can live in peace.

Staying Safe: Why Countries Have Soldiers

Sometimes, countries have soldiers. Soldiers are brave men and women who wear uniforms and help protect their country. They train very hard to be strong and helpful. Their job is to make sure that everyone inside their country's borders feels secure and no one tries to cause trouble. Think of them like very helpful security guards for a whole country! They are there to make sure everyone can go about their day, play, learn, and grow up safely. They are always thinking about how to keep their homes and communities out of harm's way. Did you know? Most countries in the world have soldiers who help keep the peace and protect their citizens. They are a big part of keeping everyone safe!

What Does "Presence" Mean?

The news said that Israel "will maintain its presence in Lebanon." This might sound like a big word, but it just means that some people from Israel, like soldiers, are staying in a part near Lebanon. They are there to watch and make sure everything is calm and peaceful. It's like if your neighbor asks you to keep an eye on their garden for a little while, to make sure no mischievous animals come in! They are "present" there. When the news says "maintain its presence," it means they want to keep these people there for now. They feel it's important for their safety. Did you know? Being "present" means being there! If you are present at school, it means you are there learning and playing with your friends.

A Chat Between Friends: USA and Israel

There's a very big country far away called the United States of America, or the USA for short. The USA is a friend to many countries around the world, including Israel. Friends often talk to each other and share their thoughts. Sometimes, one friend might suggest something to another. The USA thought it might be a good idea for Israel to "withdraw" from Lebanon. "Withdraw" means to leave or go back home. So, the USA was suggesting that the Israeli people near Lebanon should go back to Israel. It's like when your friend tells you, "Maybe you should come play inside now." They are suggesting you do something different. Did you know? The USA is a huge country with many states, and it's famous for places like Hollywood and big cities! It talks to countries all over the world, trying to help make things better.

Why Does Israel Want to Stay?

The Defence Minister, Mr. Katz, said that Israel will "not withdraw." This means they want to keep their people and soldiers there for now. Why? Because they feel it's very important for their safety. Israel believes that having their people near Lebanon helps to keep their own country safe from any trouble. They want to make sure no one tries to hurt the people living in Israel. They feel that staying there helps them watch out for any dangers. It's like if you saw a small crack in your fence and you wanted to stay nearby to make sure no animals get in until it's fixed. You stay because you want to keep your home safe. They are thinking about the security of their country and their people. They want to protect their families, their homes, and their future. Did you know? Everyone, no matter where they live, wants to feel safe and protected in their home and community. It's a very important feeling!

What Happens Next?

So, Defence Minister Katz from Israel has said, "We are staying here for now." This means that even if their friend the USA has a different idea, Israel believes it's best for them to keep their people near Lebanon to help keep safe. Countries, just like people, sometimes have different ideas about the best way to do things. But it's important for them to keep talking to each other. Talking helps everyone understand each other better. We hope that all the countries in the world, and especially our neighbors Israel and Lebanon, can find ways to live in peace and harmony, where everyone feels safe and happy. Did you know? Many people in Africa also wish for peace and understanding between all nations. We believe that talking and helping each other makes the world a better place for all children!

Wishing for Peace

As your friendly African reporter, I want to remind everyone that even when there are disagreements, the most important thing is to keep talking and to always wish for peace. We all want a world where children can play safely, learn happily, and grow up strong, no matter which country they live in. Let's send our happy thoughts and wishes for peace to Israel and Lebanon and to all countries around the world! That's all for today, wonderful friends! Keep smiling, keep learning, and remember to be kind to your neighbors! Bye-bye for now!
summary: Israel's Defence Minister said they will keep their soldiers near Lebanon for safety, even if their friend the USA suggests they should leave.
whyMatters: This is important because countries that are neighbors need to talk and understand each other to keep everyone safe and live in peace. It shows that even friends can have different ideas, but safety is very important for all countries.

originalTitle: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
title: Good News! Grown-ups Talk About Keeping Our World Safe!
content:
Hello from Your African News Friend!

Hello, amazing young friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa here. I have some exciting news to share with you today. It's about grown-ups from all over the world working together to make sure our big, beautiful planet stays safe and peaceful for everyone, especially for awesome kids like you! We love peace here in Africa, and we love to hear about people working together. Today's news is about special helpers and a country far away called Iran. They are talking about visits and making big plans. It might sound like grown-up stuff, but it's super important for making sure everyone can live without worries. So, let's find out what's happening!

What's This Big News About?

There's a very big club of countries called the United Nations, or UN for short. Imagine all the countries in the world holding hands! The UN helps everyone get along. This big club has special helpers. They are like super detectives for atomic power. This group is called the IAEA (say it like: Eye-A-E-A). These helpers make sure that a very strong kind of energy, called 'atomic energy' or 'nuclear energy,' is used only for good things. Atomic energy is super powerful! It can help make electricity for our lights and phones. But, if it's not handled carefully, it can also be used to make big, scary bombs. That's why the IAEA helpers are so important. They want to make sure everyone uses this power for peace, not for danger. They are like the referees in a big soccer game, making sure everyone plays fair.

Who Are the Special Helpers?

One of the main helpers leading the IAEA team is a very kind man named Mr. Rafael Grossi. He is the chief helper, like the captain of a very important team. His job is to make sure all countries follow the rules when they use atomic energy. His team has special 'inspectors.' These inspectors are like friendly detectives. They visit different places. What do these inspectors do? They look, they check, and they ask questions. They want to see that everything is being done the right way. They make sure the powerful energy is used for things like making electricity to power our homes and schools, or for special medicines that help people get better. They want to be sure it's not used for anything that could hurt anyone. Did you know? The sun, which gives us warmth and light every day, is like a giant natural atomic power station far, far away! It uses atomic energy to shine so brightly and help plants grow and gives us yummy food. So, atomic power itself can be very good!

Why Do They Want to Visit Iran?

There is a country called Iran, which is far away from Africa. Iran has some special buildings where they work with this atomic energy. The IAEA special helpers, led by Mr. Grossi, want to visit these buildings in Iran. Why? To check that Iran is using this energy only for good things, like making electricity for their people. They want to be sure Iran is not making anything that could be dangerous. It's a bit like when your teacher asks to see your homework. Not because they don't trust you, but because they want to make sure you're learning well and following the school rules. The helpers want to make sure all countries are following the world's rules for atomic energy. This helps everyone feel safe and trust each other. Trust is like a strong bridge that connects people and countries.

What's the Tricky Part?

So, Mr. Grossi, the chief helper, says, "We want to plan our visit to Iran soon!" But then, one grown-up from Iran said something a little different. This person said, "Yes, a visit can happen, but only after we make a *final* big plan with another big country, America." They want to finish all the big talks and agreements first. It's a bit like if you want to play a game with your friend right now, but your friend says, "Let's play, but only after we finish building our LEGO castle!" Both sides want to do a good thing (visit and check, or play), but they have different ideas about *when* it should happen or what needs to happen first. They are still talking to find a way that works for everyone. Talking is always the best way to solve problems, just like we learn to do with our friends and family.

Why Is This Important for Us?

This news might seem far away, but it's important for everyone on Earth, including us here in Africa! When special helpers like Mr. Grossi and his team check on atomic energy, it helps all countries feel more peaceful. When countries trust each other, there is less worry about fighting or big problems. It means less chance of anyone using atomic power for bad things. Think about it like this: when everyone in your classroom follows the rules, everyone can learn and play happily. There are no fights, and everyone feels safe. The same is true for countries! When they follow the rules about powerful energy, it helps keep the whole world a safe and happy place for all children to grow up in. We want a world full of smiles and playtime, not worries. Did you know? Many countries in Africa work together in groups like the African Union! They talk, share ideas, and help each other to keep peace and make life better for everyone across our continent. This shows how important talking and working together really are!

What Happens Next?

The grown-ups are not fighting; they are just talking and trying to understand each other better. Mr. Grossi and the people from Iran are going to keep talking. They are working hard to find a good plan for the special helpers to visit Iran's buildings. They want to make sure everyone agrees on the best way forward. Sometimes, it takes a little while for grown-ups to agree on big plans. But talking and working together is the best way to solve any puzzle. We hope they will agree very soon so that everyone can feel even safer and more peaceful.

Stay Tuned!

So, my wonderful young friends, remember that there are many good grown-ups working hard every day to keep our world safe and happy. They are making sure big, powerful things like atomic energy are used only for good. Keep smiling, keep learning, and know that people are always trying their best to build a peaceful future for you! That's all for today's news from your friend in Africa! Until next time, stay curious and keep shining bright!
summary: Special helpers from the United Nations want to visit a country called Iran to make sure they are using powerful atomic energy only for good things, like making electricity, to keep our world safe and peaceful.
whyMatters: This is important because when countries let special helpers check on powerful energy, it helps everyone trust each other, stops big problems, and keeps the world safe and peaceful for all kids to grow up in.

originalTitle: England's James and Rice to have fitness assessed
title: England's James and Rice to have fitness assessed
content: England will assess the fitness of Reece James and Declan Rice before their final World Cup group game against Panama.

originalTitle: Madibo given five-game ban for causing Kone leg break
title: Madibo given five-game ban for causing Kone leg break
content: Qatar midfielder Assim Madibo is given a five-match ban for the challenge which resulted in a broken leg for Canada's Ismael Kone.

originalTitle: England to face Scotland? World Cup knockout stage as it stands
title: England to face Scotland? World Cup knockout stage as it stands
content: Use BBC Sport's projections tool with in-game updates to plot your country's route through the World Cup knockouts.
